content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Norrine coyle 
city Clerk 
city of Modesto 
P. o. Box 642 
Modesto, CA 
95353 
Dear Ms. Coyle: 
November 15, 1989 
Re: Combined campaign Report 
Our File No. 1-89-623 
This is to confirm advice provided to your office on october 
27, 1989, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act") .1/ 
This letter constitutes writ-
ten advice under section 84205. 
section 84205 states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit 
candidates and committees to file campaign statements combin-
ing statements and reports required to be filed by this 
title. 
A special election has been called in the city of Modesto to 
fill a vacant seat on the city Council. 
The election date is 
January 30, 1990. 
Because the second pre-election statement due 
in connection with the special election (Section 84200.8) 
a~d the 
semi-annual statement due for the last six months of 1989 (Section 
84200) cover overlapping periods, we believe it is appropriate for 
candidates being voted on in that election, their controlled 
committees and committees primarily formed to support or oppose 
candidates in that election to combine those two reports. 
Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on 
the following schedule: 
1/ 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, 
seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite ROO 
• 
P.O. Box 8(\7 • 
S;l.:ranll.'I\w C.-\ t)SS('4-l"S(Y; • 
(t) I ()) ,22-S66l" 


Norrine Coyle 
Page Two 
~ 
of statement 
1st Pre-Election 
Period Covered 
Jan. 12/ -
Dec. 16 
Filing Deadline 
December 21, 1989 
2nd Pre-Election 
Dec. 17 - Jan. 13 
January 18, 1990 
Combined with semi-annual 
statement for the second 
half of 1989 
Semi-Annual 
Jan. 14 - June 30 
July 31, 1990 
The contribution limitations imposed by Proposition 73 apply 
separately for a special election and are the same amounts as the 
limitations for a fiscal year. 
A contributor may contribute up to 
the stated limits (see attached) in the special election cycle, 
less any amount contributed during the fiscal year which the 
candidate transfers to his or her special election committee bank 
account. 3 / 
For your reference, "special election cycle" means the 
day on which the office becomes vacant until the day of the 
special election. 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General/ounsel 
/~~£~ 
By: 
Lynda Cassady 
Political Reform Consultant 
2/ 
The period covered by any statement begins on the day after 
the closing date of the last statement filed or January 1 if no 
previous statement has been filed. 
3/ 
See enclosed proposed temporary order in Service employees 
international union, et ale v. Fair Political Practices 
Commission, U.S. District Court, Eastern District of California, 
Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM. 
The court will hold a hearing on 
this issue on November 15, 1989, and issue a permanent order on or 
after that date.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether candidates, committees, and controlled committees in a special election to fill a vacant City Council seat in Modesto may combine their second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989, given overlapping reporting periods.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits combining the second pre-election statement and semi-annual statement for the second half of 1989 because they cover overlapping periods. The combined report is due January 18, 1990, and contribution limitations under Proposition 73 apply separately for the special election cycle, with limits reduced by amounts contributed during the fiscal year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmed written advice under Government Code Section 84205. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; advice provided verbatim in body paragraphs. Includes detailed filing schedule table and contribution limitation tables for special election cycle. Minor formatting variations in source document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether candidates, committees, and controlled committees in a special election to fill a vacant City Council seat in Modesto may combine their second pre-election statement with the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989, given overlapping reporting periods.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC permits combining the second pre-election statement and semi-annual statement for the second half of 1989 because they cover overlapping periods. The combined report is due January 18, 1990, and contribution limitations under Proposition 73 apply separately for the special election cycle, with limits reduced by amounts contributed during the fiscal year.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that candidates and committees in the January 30, 1990 special election for a Modesto City Council vacancy may combine their second pre-election and semi-annual statements due to overlapping periods, with specific filing deadlines and contribution limitation guidance provided.
